---
applyTo: "**/*"
---

# Developer Flow
This section describes the flow developers should follow when contributing code to the repository.
Developers should follow these steps when contributing code:

0. Check which Github Issues are in the Ready box, Analyse what needs to be picked up first. some issues can be picked up together if that will save time.
1. Put the issues you will work on in In-progress
2. Always create a new repository for the work you are going to contribute. 
3. Naming conventions of new repositories are: Feature/<short-description-of-feature> Bug/<short-description-of-bug>.
4. When you are done working on an item, create a pull request from your repository to the main repository so that your changes can be reviewed and merged.
5. Put the corresponding github issue in Review tab.
6. After the pull request has been reviewed and approved, and the changes have been merged into the main branch, move the corresponding GitHub issue to the Done column.

# Rules
- Never commit directly to main
- You can not approve the pull request, this has to be done by a human unless specificly told otherwise by a human.
